{{Quote|Are you capable of taking the initiative, Captain?|[[Ysanne Isard]]|<ref name="X-Wing: The Bacta War">''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''</ref>}}
'''Lakwii Varrscha''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] and an officer in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]—she was a tall, muscular woman who seemed diligent but was generally regarded by her superiors to be no more than ordinarily competent.

==Biography==
Early in her career, Varrscha had been assigned to [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]], where she served as the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of a [[Imperial Customs Corvette|light corvette]]. Subsequently, she transferred to the line forces of the Navy, and for several years, she served aboard the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Errant Venture/Legends|''Virulence'']] under the command of [[Joak Drysso]], where she achieved the post of executive officer with the rank of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]]. When Drysso was transferred to command the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', she was advanced to replace him, and she commanded the ''Virulence'' during the [[Bacta War]], until [[Booster Terrik]] bluffed her into surrendering at [[Yag-prime|Yag'Dhul Station]].

Most people who encountered Captain Varrscha were left with the impression that she was somewhat uninspired. [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax Terrik]], who had outrun her corvette once, recalled her tactics as completely conventional, while [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Sair Yonka]] of the Star Destroyer [[Freedom|''Avarice'']] noted that the performance of ''Virulence'' under her command was unimpressive: in particular, she had failed to improve her [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]]' performance when she knew that she would be faced with [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. [[Fliry Vorru]] thought that she had risen as far as she had simply by standing at Drysso's side, and was concerned that command of a Star Destroyer taxed her to the limits of her ability. But considering her willingness to surrender the ''Virulence'' at Yag'Dhul, another interpretation of her lack of zeal is also possible—Lakwii Varrscha was simply not fully committed to the Imperial service.

==Appearances==
*''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: The Bacta War (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}}
